message:
feat(net/unix): implement UnixStream with AsyncRead/AsyncWrite and splitting

Add full async I/O support to UnixStream:
- AsyncRead/AsyncWrite trait implementations using Phase 0 yield pattern
- split() for borrowed read/write halves with lifetime tracking
- into_split() for owned halves that can be moved to separate tasks
- reunite() to recombine owned halves with Arc pointer equality check
- connect_abstract() for Linux abstract namespace sockets
- ReuniteError type for failed reunification attempts

New split.rs module provides ReadHalf, WriteHalf (borrowed) and
OwnedReadHalf, OwnedWriteHalf (owned) types matching tokio's API.

All 27 unix-related tests pass.

Closes: asupersync-lhk5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
